description: Two code paths acquire the same two locks in opposite orders, wedging under concurrency.
body: Failure mode: path one locks A then B while path two locks B then A, so when they run concurrently each holds one lock and waits forever for the other, hanging the threads (and often the whole process). Detection signals: the process hangs under load; a thread dump shows a cycle in the wait-for graph; two stacks each hold one lock and block on the other. Do not add a second lock to a path without placing it in the global order. Safer fallback: define a total lock order by rank, acquire strictly in increasing rank everywhere, or use try-lock with backoff, then confirm an acyclic wait-for graph and a non-hanging stress test.
